proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +42.5pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Portfolio is 100% UP_TRI (6/6 open); if Bear re-emerges quickly, this promotion could concentrate signal-type exposure further — monitor by_signal_type concentration on next cycle
D-8 R-multiple review outstanding (Day-6 forced-exit dominance) — R=0.61 may reflect exit-policy artifact rather than true edge decay
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +42.5pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +42.5pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61 below the Tier S threshold. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 5), so the cohort is dormant and portfolio's 100% UP_TRI / 66.7% Choppy exposure is not compounded by a Bear-gated promotion. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-07-14) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: BULL_PROXY × Bear: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +29.9pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +29.9pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +29.9pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 5), so it is dormant; portfolio has 0 Bear exposure and no regime concentration triggered (0.6667 below 0.80), so promotion does not compound risk and may diversify when Bear re-emerges. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-06-19 through 07-14) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_signal_type + by_signal_type×regime×sector = UP_TRI (100%); UP_TRI×Choppy×Metal (33.33%) (100.0%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_signal_type + by_signal_type×regime×sector = UP_TRI (100%); UP_TRI×Choppy×Metal (33.33%); recommended action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI and specifically block additions to sector=Metal under Choppy regime; allow only non-UP_TRI signals or natural Day-6 exits to reduce monoculture before adding directional long risk.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: If Choppy regime breaks to a Bear shock or trend reversal, 100% UP_TRI directional long book takes correlated drawdown with no offsetting exposure; the 2-position Metal cluster amplifies sector-specific tail loss with no validated cohort edge to justify the concentration.

evidence: matched cohort Choppy×UP_TRI at Candidate tier (n=294) — unvalidated edge
matched cohort Bull×UP_TRI at Candidate tier (n=49) — unvalidated edge
regime mix Choppy 66.7% / Bull 33.3% — no dominant validated regime backing
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI and specifically block additions to sector=Metal under Choppy regime; allow only non-UP_TRI signals or natural Day-6 exits to reduce monoculture before adding directional long risk.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: If Choppy regime breaks to a Bear shock or trend reversal, 100% UP_TRI directional long book takes correlated drawdown with no offsetting exposure; the 2-position Metal cluster amplifies sector-specific tail loss with no validated cohort edge to justify the concentration.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: UP_TRI is 100% of the book (6/6 = 1.0, well above the 0.85 signal-type threshold) making it a pure monoculture directional long, AND the UP_TRI×Choppy×Metal cell breaches the 0.20 cell threshold at 33.33% (2 of 6 positions). Matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort sits only at Candidate tier (n=294) — unvalidated edge — and regime_watch context is unstable with a Choppy/Bull mix (Choppy 66.7%, Bull 33.3%). Concentration is unvalidated exposure, not validated edge.
